description:
A conceptual design engineer is reviewing a regional transport aircraft model. The flight dynamics team has determined that the horizontal tail is undersized. You must calculate the new required tail area to achieve a Horizontal Tail Volume Coefficient (Vh) of exactly 1.10, and update the parametric model.

Expected Actions:
1. Open /home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/aircraft_configuration.vsp3 in OpenVSP.
2. Select the 'Wing' component. From its Plan and Design tabs, read and record the Wing Area (Sw), Mean Aerodynamic Chord (MACw), and X-Location (Xw).
3. Select the 'Horiz_Tail' component. From its Design tab, read the Tail X-Location (Xt).
4. Calculate the moment arm (lh) = Xt - Xw.
5. Calculate the Required Tail Area (St) to achieve Vh = 1.10 using the formula: St = (1.10 * Sw * MACw) / lh
6. In the Horiz_Tail's Plan tab, change the Area to the newly calculated St. IMPORTANT: You must ensure the tail's Aspect Ratio (AR) remains unchanged (lock it in the UI before changing Area).
7. Save the modified model to /home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/aircraft_configuration_sized.vsp3.
8. Write a report to /home/ga/Desktop/sizing_report.txt documenting your values.

Your report MUST follow this exact format:
Wing Area (Sw): [value]
Wing MAC (MACw): [value]
Wing X-Loc (Xw): [value]
Tail X-Loc (Xt): [value]
Moment Arm (lh): [value]
Required Tail Area (St): [value]
